name: Senolytics Coverage Repair Assessment for June 11, 2026

assessment_window.basis: Coverage repair created after the June 11 assessment found that the published Senolytics outlook was missing direct source, study, finding, trial-watch, review, seminal-anchor, and support-map coverage.

summary: Coverage is now adequate for a moderate-confidence human_biomarker_signal Senolytics public claim, pending review and publication of the staged repair bundle. The repair stages D+Q discovery and old-mouse healthspan/lifespan anchors, direct human D+Q IPF, DKD, postmenopausal bone, and cognition/mobility records, a translation-review safety boundary, fisetin discovery context, active no-results fisetin and D+Q registry watch records, and support-map normalization for the existing D+Q endothelial TIF animal record. Coverage is not strong because durable human functional benefit, broad healthy-aging prevention, disease modification, long-term safety, and senescent-cell specificity remain unresolved.

evidence_categories.rationale: The repair stages direct human D+Q records across IPF open-label function, DKD senescent-cell biomarkers, randomized IPF tolerability, postmenopausal bone RCT biomarkers, and a 2025 cognition/mobility pilot, plus active D+Q and fisetin registry-watch records.
evidence_categories.gap_note: The remaining issue is evidence strength rather than coverage: human functional and disease-modification signals remain preliminary, small, mixed, or no-results.

evidence_categories.rationale: The staged public claim depends on interventional senolytic studies, animal/mechanistic anchors, and registry watch rather than observational or natural-experiment evidence.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Future safety surveillance could use observational cohorts if off-label preventive use becomes common, but that is not required for the current support map.

evidence_categories.rationale: The repair stages the Achilles heel discovery paper, the 2018 old-mouse healthspan/lifespan D+Q paper, and the existing aged-mouse endothelial TIF result as direct animal support.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Animal support is adequate for rationale, but it remains translation support rather than human longevity evidence.

evidence_categories.rationale: The repair stages senescent-cell pro-survival pathway discovery, fisetin cell-type specificity, human senescent-cell/SASP biomarker movement, and review-level translation context.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Mechanistic target engagement still needs tissue-specific human replication and linkage to durable functional outcomes.

evidence_categories.rationale: The support map now foregrounds the randomized IPF feasibility trial's lack of meaningful exploratory functional separation, the postmenopausal bone RCT's null primary endpoint, no-results registries, and the review-level caution against use outside trials.

The repair improves safety coverage with D+Q IPF, bone RCT, cognition/mobility pilot, and review context, but long-term preventive-use safety, off-target toxicity, tissue-specific clearance risk, durability, and intermittent dosing risk-benefit remain thin.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Upgrade only after larger, longer, dose-specific human trials show acceptable safety and durable aging-relevant benefit.

evidence_categories.rationale: The repair stages current no-results watch records for AFFIRM, AFFIRM-LITE, fisetin vascular function, and SToMP-AD phase 2, and records posted-result context for NCT05422885 and NCT04313634 in the session and study details.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Continue surveillance for posted results, status changes, and linked publications, especially NCT03430037, NCT03675724, NCT06133634, NCT04685590, and NCT04785300.

evidence_categories.rationale: The repair stages the direct senolytic-drugs discovery-to-translation review as the review-level safety and clinical-boundary anchor, while newer result-bearing human papers carry the current evidence update.
evidence_categories.gap_note: A later review-refresh pass could add post-2024 syntheses, but the current public claim no longer lacks review-level boundary support.

evidence_categories.rationale: The Achilles heel discovery paper, fisetin/BCL-XL discovery paper, and old-mouse D+Q healthspan/lifespan paper now anchor core D+Q and fisetin branch history.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Future branch-specific repairs could add navitoclax or BCL-family records if the public scope expands beyond D+Q and fisetin.

evidence_categories.rationale: The staged outlook separates senolytics from senomorphics, immune clearance, telomere-protection, BCL-family, fisetin, D+Q, and disease-specific trial claims.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Future publication and surveillance should keep D+Q, fisetin, BCL-family agents, immune-clearance therapies, and senomorphic agents separate unless direct comparative evidence supports class-level claims.

search_log_summary: Used PubMed ESummary/EFetch and ClinicalTrials.gov API v2 on June 11, 2026, with local comparison against the Senolytics outlook, prior surveillance session, previous June 11 coverage assessment, existing D+Q records, and neighboring support maps.
source_selection_notes: Staged result-bearing human D+Q records and representative current no-results D+Q/fisetin registries rather than every disease-specific senolytic registry hit.
Kept evidence_stage at human_biomarker_signal because controlled human evidence is biomarker-heavy and mixed; functional signals remain exploratory.
Included the existing D+Q endothelial TIF record as a staged update so Senolytics gets direct support while retaining the telomere-protection boundary.

known_gaps.description: No replicated adequately powered randomized human trial yet shows durable functional, disease, frailty, cognition, mobility, morbidity, or mortality benefit from senolytics.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Watch posted results and publications from D+Q and fisetin trials; require prespecified functional outcomes before stage upgrade.

known_gaps.description: Current human safety coverage is short, small, and disease-specific; chronic or repeated preventive-use safety remains unresolved.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Keep safety limitations in editorial review and surveillance; do not strengthen public claims until longer controlled safety data are available.

known_gaps.description: Direct fisetin older-women frailty, older-adult inflammation, and vascular-function trials remain no-results in the June 11, 2026 ClinicalTrials.gov view.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Recheck NCT03430037, NCT03675724, and NCT06133634 during ordinary surveillance.

known_gaps.description: The repair stages a direct 2020 translation review plus newer result-bearing papers, but a later post-2024 review synthesis could improve review recency.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Consider a review-refresh pass only after the staged repair is reviewed and if the public narrative needs synthesis beyond the current support map.

known_gaps.description: D+Q, fisetin, BCL-family agents, and immune-mediated senescent-cell clearance may affect different senescent-cell populations and tissues.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Maintain branch-specific support maps and avoid class-wide claims unless direct comparative or mechanistic target-engagement evidence supports them.

next_coverage_action: Run evidence review on senolytics-coverage-repair-2026-06-11; if approved and published, return Senolytics to ordinary surveillance with watch on current no-results D+Q and fisetin trials.
